skill.name: expo-deployment
skill.description: Deploy Expo React Native apps to production environments with app store submission and OTA updates
skill.summary: Complete guidance for deploying Expo apps to iOS App Store, Google Play, and managing over-the-air updates
skill.author: sickn33

content.value_statement: Deploying Expo apps to production requires complex build configuration and app store compliance. This skill provides expert guidance for EAS Build, app store submission, and over-the-air updates.
actual_capabilities: Configure EAS Build for production iOS and Android builds
Guide app store metadata preparation for App Store Connect and Google Play Console
Set up over-the-air update channels with EAS Update
Manage release channels and staged rollout strategies
Optimize bundle size and production build settings
Handle app review requirements and compliance issues
limitations: Does not execute builds or submit to app stores directly
Requires valid Apple Developer and Google Play Console accounts
Cannot access your Expo account or project credentials
Does not replace manual app store review process

use_cases.title: First-Time App Store Submission
use_cases.description: Prepare and submit your Expo app to iOS App Store and Google Play for the first time with complete configuration guidance.
use_cases.target_user: Mobile developers launching their first production app

use_cases.title: OTA Update Pipeline
use_cases.description: Configure over-the-air updates for rapid bug fixes and feature releases without app store resubmission.
use_cases.target_user: Teams managing active production apps with frequent updates

use_cases.title: Enterprise Release Management
use_cases.description: Implement staged rollouts, release channels, and production monitoring for enterprise-grade deployments.
use_cases.target_user: Engineering leads managing multiple app versions and environments

prompt_templates.title: Basic Deployment Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up my Expo app for production deployment. I need to configure EAS Build for both iOS and Android, and prepare for app store submission.
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer ready to deploy their first production build

prompt_templates.title: App Store Metadata Preparation
prompt_templates.prompt: What metadata and assets do I need to prepare for submitting my Expo app to the Apple App Store and Google Play Store? Provide a complete checklist.
prompt_templates.scenario: Preparing app store listings for initial submission

prompt_templates.title: OTA Update Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure over-the-air updates for my Expo app. I need separate channels for production, staging, and development with proper rollout strategies.
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up EAS Update for continuous deployment

prompt_templates.title: Production Build Troubleshooting
prompt_templates.prompt: My EAS Build is failing with configuration errors. Review my app.json and eas.json settings and identify issues preventing successful production builds.

output_examples.output: Step-by-step EAS Build configuration including eas.json setup with production profile, build credentials configuration, and commands to trigger production builds for iOS and Android platforms.

Complete EAS Update configuration with production, staging, and development channels, including update group management, rollout percentage settings, and commands to publish updates to specific channels.

best_practices: Use EAS Build for reliable and reproducible production builds
Test production builds on physical devices before app store submission
Implement proper error tracking and analytics before going live
anti_patterns: Skipping production build testing and submitting untested binaries
Using development configuration in production builds
Publishing OTA updates to production channel without staging validation

faq.question: What do I need before deploying my Expo app to production?
faq.answer: You need an Apple Developer account for iOS deployment, a Google Play Console account for Android, configured EAS Build credentials, and a tested production build. Ensure all environment variables are set for production.

faq.question: How long does app store review take?
faq.answer: Apple App Store review typically takes 24-48 hours for initial submissions. Google Play review is usually faster, often within a few hours. First-time submissions may take longer for both platforms.

faq.question: What is the difference between EAS Build and OTA updates?
faq.answer: EAS Build creates native binaries for app store submission with native code changes. OTA updates deliver JavaScript and asset changes instantly without app store review, but cannot modify native code.

faq.question: Can I rollback a bad OTA update?
faq.answer: Yes, EAS Update supports automatic rollback. You can configure rollback thresholds based on crash rates, and manually rollback by republishing the previous update group to the same channel.

faq.question: Do I need separate builds for different environments?
faq.answer: Yes, use EAS Build channels or build profiles to create separate production, staging, and development builds. Each should have appropriate API endpoints and configuration for its environment.

faq.question: How do I handle app store rejection?
faq.answer: Review the rejection reason in App Store Connect or Play Console. Common issues include missing metadata, guideline violations, or technical problems. Fix the issue and resubmit with a response explaining the changes.
